rulings.Case Title: CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL, LTD., A FOREIGN CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendant Todd Theodora'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents Set One
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 9, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 1, Cross Defendant Todd Theodora's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents Set One Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. It would be helpful to the court for the parties to be prepared to address the question of whether, or to what extent, Cross Complainant Chahine has put privileged communications directly at issue under Mitchell v. Superior Court (1974) 37 Cal. App. 3d 606, 609. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL, LTD., A FOREIGN CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendant Todd Theodora'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interoggatories Set One
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 9, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 1, Cross Defendant Todd Theodora's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interoggatories Set One Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART. GRANTED with respect to Cross-Complainant Chahine's response to Form Interrogatory 17.1 in connection with Cross-Complainant Chahine's denial of request for Admission Nos. 13, 14, 15, and 20. DENIED in all other respects. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL, LTD., A FOREIGN CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendant Todd Theodora'S Motion To Special Interoggatories Set One
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 9, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 1, Cross Defendant Todd Theodora's Motion To Special Interoggatories Set One Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. Cross-Complainant Chahine's incorporation by reference of other special interrogatory responses without summarizing or quoting from the information does not meet the requirements of Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 Cal. App. 3d 771, 783. Moreover, Cross-Complainant's answers that incorporate a reference to documents do not comply with C.C.P. Section 2030.230 because, according to Cross-Complainant's Chahine's own responses, a compilation, abstract, audit , or summary of the records already exists in the referenced documents. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL, LTD., A FOREIGN CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Todd Theodora To Appear For Deposition And Produce Docs; Memo Of P&A In Support Of Thereof And Declaration Of Robert A. Schwartz;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 9, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANT MARTIN BROS./MARCOWALL, INC., MOHAMMAD CHAHINE'S Motion To Compel Todd Theodora To Appear For Deposition And Produce Documents. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Weixel as follows: Denied. The deposition of Todd Theodora is noticed to reconvene on December 11 and 12, 2012, and there is no good cause shown to compel an earlier deposition. The motion to compel production of documents is premature as Mr. Theodora's deposition has not yet reconvened. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ROBERTO LOPEZ et al VS. MICHAEL BERGMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 9, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 3, DEFENDANT MICHAEL BERGMAN'S Notice Of Motion For Terminating Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied without prejudice. The Court sets a hearing for December 11, 2012 at 9:00 for an order to show cause why terminating sanctions should not be granted in the event plaintiff has not fully complied with the court's order entered Aug 29, 2012 before the hearing date. Any brief or response is limited to 5 pages, plus documentary evidence, and shall be filed and served in accordance with CCP § 1005(b). Courtesy copies are to be provided to Department 302 at the time of filing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. Black and White Checker Cab shall bring to the hearing copies of all of the discovery orders that are subject to Mr. Soroori's non-compliance and/or referred to in moving party's motion. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MA ELOISA CATU VS. SAFEWAY, INC. A CORPORATION, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Pltf'S Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf And Her Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 9, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 6, DEFENDANT SAFEWAY INC.'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Discovery Responses And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Her Counsel Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. By failing to respond to the discovery propounded within the time limits provided in the Code of Civil Procedure, Plaintiff has waived all objections. Within 30 days, Plaintiff shall serve verified, code-compliant responses without objections, to Safeway's Requests for Production of Documents, Set One; Safeway's Special Interrogatories, Set One; and Safeway's Form Interrogatories - General, Set One. Safeway's request for sanctions is GRANTED IN PART, in the amount of $740 plus $80 = $820. Within 30 days, Plaintiff shall pay Safeway $820.00 Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: GRACIELA CANIZALEZ VS. HECTOR NAVARRO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Futher Responses To Req.'S For Admissions (Set One); Memo Of P & A
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 9, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 7, DEFENDANTS HECTOR NAVARRO, ANTHONY NAVARRO, NAVARRO FAMILY TRUST'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request's For Admissions (Set One) Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART. The motion is timely because the responses were served by mail and thus the deadline for making a motion is extended under C.C.P. 1013(a) by 5 calendar days. Defendants' motion is DENIED with respect to all Plaintiffs' response to RFA #6. They are adequate because, although a qualified admission, the responses state the basis for the qualification. The motion is GRANTED with respect to Plaintiffs Graciela, Dalia, and Erlinda Canizalez's responses to RFA Nos. 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, and 18 because the responses stated simply, "Qualified Admission" without stating the basis for qualifying the admission. The motion is GRANTED with respect to Plaintiff Graciela Canizalez's response to RFA No. 27, and Plaintiffs Dalia and Erlinda Canizalez's responses to RFA Nos. 20. These responses state an inability to admit or deny without meeting the requirement of C.C.P. Sectiopn 2033.220(b)(3) and (c). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 9, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 8, DEFENDANT WSF BEVERAGE CORPORATION'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Exam Of Plaintiff Viliana Atanasova Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. rulings.Case Title: ROUSDEM DJORAEV et al VS. KONSTANTIN TSAPLIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Order That Req For Admission Set One Deemed Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 9, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 9, DEFENDANT KONSTANTIN TSAPLIN'S Motion For Order That Request For Admission Set One Deemed Admitted Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. The requests for admissions - set one served on plaintiff Rousdem Djoraev are DEEMED ADMITTED. Sanctions are granted pursuant to C.C.P. 2033.280(c) in the amount of $550.00 plus $60.00 for motion filing fees. rulings.Case Title: PAUL ALLEN et al VS. GEICO INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 9, 2012, Line 22, DEFENDANT GEICO INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Court overrules the demurrer to the breach of contract and bad faith causes of action. Defendant's moving papers focus on the umbrella policy and ignore plaintiff's allegations that he is entitled to greater benefits under the primary/automobile policy. It is true that Insurance Code § 11580.2(m) provides a coverage floor, but the policy-which is not before the Court-may provide for greater coverage. See Calfarm Ins. Co. v. Wolf (2001) 86 Cal.App.4th 811, 817; Utah Prop & Cas. Ins Guar. Ass'n v. United Services Auto Ass'n (1991) 230 Cal.App.3d 1010, 1020. The Court cannot determine that plaintiff's claim fails at this stage of the litigation. The Court sustains the demurrer to the negligent misrepresentation and negligence causes of action for plaintiff to plead more specific allegations. See Wallman v. Suddock (2011) 200 Cal. App. 4th 1288, 1308-1315. The Court overrules the demurrer to the negligence per se cause of action because defendant only references the umbrella policy in the demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)
